topic: In China employee termination: control-tower payroll execution for foreign employers
related_keywords: China termination SOP for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Establishes framework for lawful termination, severance exposure, and compensation risk.
Supports requirement for consistent legal basis and documentation in termination handling.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Anchors social insurance obligations affecting termination month payroll and offboarding closure.
Supports timeline controls for contribution/finalization consistency.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Provides practical context for payroll tax treatment and annual reconciliation environment.
Supports documenting assumptions for final-pay component handling.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Multinational employers need process and documentation discipline in China employment decisions.
Execution quality is a core determinant of dispute exposure.

consensus: Termination risk for foreign employers is reduced when legal basis, payroll mapping, and communication are controlled as one workflow.
Evidence completeness and timeline discipline are central to defensible outcomes.
Exception routing before payroll lock is more reliable than ad hoc post-run correction.
uncertainties: City-level adjudication and authority practice may differ on edge-case evidence sufficiency.
Specific treatment details for certain components may require local confirmation.
English-language summaries may not reflect all Chinese-language implementation nuances.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Use a termination control-tower scoreboard at D-3 and D+7 to make in-cycle vs off-cycle decisions explicit and auditable.
